_comment_top: Merge this snippet into your existing ~/.claude/settings.json under 'hooks'. Do NOT replace existing hooks; add this entry to the PostToolUse array. After merging, validate: python3 -m json.tool ~/.claude/settings.json > /dev/null
_comment_security: Replace the placeholders with absolute paths. Do NOT use $HOME or ~ in the command string — Claude Code expands these via the shell, and a hostile $HOME (e.g. set by a project .envrc the user sourced) can redirect the hook to attacker-controlled Python. Hardcoding the full path to your Python interpreter and the wrapper script eliminates the env-poisoning vector.
_comment_python: PYTHON_PATH must be an absolute path to a Python >= 3.10 interpreter (the wrapper exec's the vendored hook with this same Python). Example: /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.13
_comment_brain: BRAIN_HOOK is the absolute path to agentic_post_tool_global.py inside your installed brain. After running install.sh, this is <your-home>/.agent/harness/hooks/agentic_post_tool_global.py — substitute literally, no $HOME.
